text:
Complete Action Plan — Cycle 3 Updated

Your Complete Dinner Planning Guide

Exact steps, phone scripts, email templates, and dietary guidance. All contact information and hours verified March 2026. Three corrections from prior cycles, two new event alerts added.

Cycle 3 Corrections (March 13, 2026):
  • NEW — Event Alert: Vancouver International Auto Show at Convention Centre (adjacent to Waterfront Station) runs March 25–29, open until 9pm on March 27. Gastown area will be busier than a typical Friday. Book in advance — walk-ins harder than expected.
  • NEW — Event Alert: Monster Jam at Pacific Coliseum (PNE), March 27 at 7pm. Expo and Millennium lines will be busier than normal eastbound from downtown after dinner. Factor this into late-night transit plans.
  • Corrected: Torafuku dinner service is Wed–Sun 5pm–9:30pm — NOT 10:30pm as stated in previous cycles. For a 7pm group booking, this works but the kitchen closes early.
  • Corrected: MeeT Yaletown Friday hours are until midnight — NOT 11pm as stated in previous cycles.
  • Confirmed: Cherry Blossom Festival "Blossoms After Dark" at David Lam Park begins March 27 — pleasant atmosphere, no downtown traffic impact.

March 27 Event Alerts — Read Before Booking

Two concurrent events on March 27 were not factored into prior planning. Neither prevents a great dinner, but they change the walk-in landscape and transit pattern.

Vancouver International Auto Show — Vancouver Convention Centre West, 1055 Canada Place (adjacent to Waterfront Station). March 25–29. Open Friday until 9pm. Attendance of 40,000+ over the weekend. The walk from the Convention Centre to Gastown restaurants is 5–10 minutes — foot traffic in the Waterfront/Gastown zone will be significantly elevated. This raises demand at popular restaurants and makes walk-in seating harder to get on Friday evening. This strengthens the case for having a confirmed reservation before March 27.
Monster Jam — Pacific Coliseum, PNE. Friday March 27 at 7pm. No direct impact on downtown dining, but eastbound Expo and Millennium lines will carry Monster Jam crowds from downtown between approximately 9pm and midnight. Guests departing toward Burnaby, Coquitlam, Surrey, or New Westminster after dinner may encounter busier-than-normal SkyTrain cars.
1
Step-by-Step Action Plan
Steps 1 and 2 must happen today, March 13
1
Survey Your Guests for Dietary Restrictions
Do This First — Before Calling Any Restaurant

The answer to this survey determines which venue you call first. Send it before you pick up the phone.

Message to Send Your Group Chat Now
Hey everyone — organizing our group dinner for Friday March 27 in Vancouver. Before I book, quick question:

Does anyone have dietary restrictions or food allergies I should flag? (vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, halal, nut allergy, shellfish, dairy-free, etc.)

Also: does anyone not eat meat?

Reply by tonight if you can — I want to book the right place. Thanks!
How to read the responses:
Any halal requirement — Nuba Gastown is the only strong central halal option in this group. Call them first.
Any vegan or severe allergy (nut, gluten) — MeeT Yaletown: peanut-free facility, dedicated GF fryers, entire menu is egg-free and dairy-free. Safest option by a wide margin for complex dietary needs.
Mixed omnivore group, no complex needs — Nuba Gastown or Torafuku. Both handle mixed groups well.
Want a special occasion feel with a private space — Homer Street Cafe.
2
Call Restaurants — In This Priority Order
Call Today (March 13) — Best Window: 2pm–5pm

Call in this order. Move to the next if unavailable. The Auto Show adds demand pressure in Gastown on March 27 — book today. Best time to call is 2–5pm, after lunch service and before dinner prep.

Call First
Nuba Gastown
Lebanese — Feel-Good Food
T604-688-1655
@nubagastown@gmail.com
+207 W Hastings St — 10 min walk from Waterfront Station
Vegan GF Options Halal Groups 8+
Friday: 11:30am – 10:30pm
Best for: Any dietary mix. Mezze sharing makes ordering easy. Bookable via Resy and OpenTable. Note: Auto Show foot traffic means walk-inis harder — reservation is more important than usual this Friday.
MeeT in Yaletown
100% Vegan — Comfort Food
T604-696-1165
@info@eatmeet.ca
+1165 Mainland St — 5 min from Yaletown-Roundhouse (Canada Line)
100% Vegan Peanut-Free Facility Dedicated GF Fryers Egg & Dairy Free
Friday: 11am – midnight (corrected from prior: not 11pm)
Best for: Vegan-heavy or serious allergy group. Yaletown location only — Gastown location closed November 2025. Wrap-around bar, covered heated patio, largest of their locations.
Homer Street Cafe & Bar
New Canadian — Michelin — Rotisserie
T604-428-4299
@info@homerstreetcafebar.com
+898 Homer St — 7 min walk from Yaletown-Roundhouse
GF Options Semi-Private Room Groups up to 14
Friday: 11:30am – 11pm
Best for: Special occasion, semi-private dining room with reclaimed windows and bespoke seating. Book via Tock or call direct. Budget $60–90/person. Michelin-recognized rotisserie kitchen.
Hours Corrected
Torafuku
Pan-Asian — Michelin-Recognized
T778-903-2006
@letseat@torafuku.ca
+958 Main St — near Main Street-Science World Station (Expo Line)
Veg Options GF Options Groups 7+ Dinner until 9:30pm only
Dinner: Wed–Sun, 5pm–9:30pm (NOT 10:30pm — prior error corrected)
Best for: Impressive omni group. Note: dinner close at 9:30pm is earlier than other options — arrive on time. Main Street-Science World station, not Waterfront — adjust transit directions for guests.
North Shore guests: SeaBus to Waterfront, then Expo Line one stop to Main Street-Science World, or 10-min Uber from Waterfront. Factor this into your invite.
La Casita Gastown
Mexican — Gastown — Late Night
T604-646-2444
@info@lacasita.ca
+101 W Cordova St — 12 min walk from Waterfront Station
Vegan Options GF Options Groups up to 75
Friday: 11:30am – 1am
Best for: Late dinner (9pm+), very large groups, or if you want to start early and linger. Private event spaces available. Open very late if the evening runs long — a strong fallback option.
Belgard Kitchen
Tapas — Urban Winery — Railtown
T604-699-1989
@reservations@belgardkitchen.com
+55 Dunlevy Ave, Railtown — 15 min walk or short Uber from Waterfront
Vegan Options GF Options Mezzanine Room
Friday: 11:30am – midnight
Best for: Private mezzanine room (12–18 seated), urban winery atmosphere. Farther from transit — requires a short Uber or 15-min walk from Waterfront. Good choice if you want the group to feel like you're somewhere special without fine-dining prices.
Guu Otokomae
Japanese Izakaya — Gastown
T604-685-8682
Wguu-izakaya.com
+#105–375 Water St, Gastown — 8 min walk from Waterfront Station
GF Options Groups OK Lively
Friday: 11:30am – midnight
Best for: Lively sharing-plates atmosphere, late-night flexibility. Not ideal for quieter conversation. Auto Show foot traffic increases chance the area is busy — call ahead for the group size.
Cycle 2: Event Unverified
Brix & Mortar
Canadian Fine Dining — Yaletown
T604-915-9463
Wbrixandmortar.ca
+1137 Hamilton St, Yaletown — 7 min from Yaletown-Roundhouse
GF Available No confirmed Mar 27 special event
Friday: 4pm – 11pm
Note: Cycle 1 docs claimed a "Night in New Orleans" event here on March 27 — no evidence of this exists. Treat as standard fine-dining only. Good restaurant, but deprioritized without the event premium.
Phone Script — What to Say When They Answer
"Hi, I'm looking to make a reservation for a group dinner. I need a table for [8–10 people] on Friday, March 27th — ideally around 7pm. Do you have availability for a group that size?

[If yes] Great. We have a couple of guests with dietary restrictions — [mention specifics: e.g., 'one vegan, one guest with a gluten intolerance']. Can the kitchen accommodate those?

[If yes] What do you need to hold the reservation? Is there a deposit or credit card hold for groups?

[If unavailable at 7pm] Do you have anything at 5:30 or 8:30pm? Could you put me on a cancellation list for 7pm?"
Email Script — For Group Reservation Requests
Subject: Group Dinner Reservation Request — March 27 (8–10 guests)

Hi,

I'd like to book a table for 8–10 guests on Friday, March 27, 2026, ideally at 7:00pm (7:30pm also works).

Our group has the following dietary requirements:
— [List from your survey, e.g., "1 vegan, 1 guest requiring gluten-free, remainder omnivore"]

Could you confirm availability and let me know what's needed to hold the reservation?

Thank you,
[Your name] — [Phone number]
3
Confirm Reservation and Send Transit Details to Guests
March 14–16 — After Hearing Back from Restaurant
Guest Confirmation Message Template
We're confirmed! Dinner details:

When: Friday, March 27 at 7pm (please arrive by 6:55)
Where: [Restaurant Name], [Full Address]

Getting there:
— From North Shore: Bus to Lonsdale Quay, SeaBus to Waterfront, then walk or short Uber. Last SeaBus back is around 12:15am.
— From Surrey / New West / Burnaby: Expo Line. Waterfront for Gastown venues. Main Street-Science World for Torafuku.
— From Coquitlam / Port Moody: Millennium Line to Waterfront.
— From Richmond / Airport: Canada Line to Yaletown-Roundhouse (MeeT, Homer St) or Waterfront.
— From Kitsilano / UBC: Bus 99-B or Bus 4 downtown.

Heads up: the Vancouver Auto Show is on this week at the Convention Centre — Waterfront area will be busier than usual. Give yourself an extra few minutes if you're walking from the station.

Use translink.ca trip planner for exact directions. See you there!
Transit note for March 27: Eastbound SkyTrain (toward Burnaby, Coquitlam, Surrey) will be busier than usual between 9pm and midnight due to Monster Jam at the PNE ending. If guests are heading east by SkyTrain, let them know to expect fuller trains and plan accordingly.
4
Confirm Final Headcount with Restaurant
March 24–25 — 48–72 Hours Before Dinner
Headcount Confirmation Call Script
"Hi, this is [name] — I have a reservation for [X] people on Friday March 27 at 7pm. I'm confirming the headcount is now [exact number]. Our dietary restrictions are [list]. Is there anything else you need before Friday?"
  • Confirm exact headcount — most restaurants need 48 hrs notice for group size changes
  • Re-confirm all dietary restrictions verbally — ask to have them noted in the booking
  • Ask if any pre-ordering is required for your party size
  • Confirm whether auto-gratuity applies (typically 18–20% for groups of 6+)
5
The Day Of — March 27
Friday, March 27
  • Send a morning reminder with the restaurant name, full address, and a Google Maps link
  • Remind guests the Auto Show is on nearby — transit at Waterfront may be busier between 5pm and 9pm
  • Arrive 10minutes early to check in at the host stand and confirm seating
  • Mention dietary restrictions again to your server — don't assume the kitchen was told at booking
  • Remind North Shore guests about the last SeaBus (~12:15am Friday night)
  • If anyone is heading east (Burnaby/Coquitlam/Surrey), note Monster Jam crowds on SkyTrain until about midnight
  • Check the bill — most restaurants auto-add 18–20% gratuity for groups of 6+
?
Restaurant Decision Guide
Use dietary survey results to pick the right venue

Q
Quick Reference
All verified March 13, 2026 — orange text = correction from prior cycle

Friday Closing Times (Corrected)

  • Nuba Gastown: 10:30pm
  • MeeT Yaletown: midnight (was 11pm)
  • Homer Street Cafe: 11pm
  • Torafuku dinner: 9:30pm (was 10:30pm)
  • Belgard Kitchen: midnight
  • Guu Otokomae: midnight
  • Brix & Mortar: 11pm (opens 4pm)
  • La Casita: 1am

March 27 Context

  • Auto Show at Convention Centre: March 25–29, open to 9pm
  • Monster Jam at PNE: starts 7pm — eastbound SkyTrain busier 9pm–midnight
  • Cherry Blossom Festival begins: David Lam Park (no downtown impact)
  • No Canucks home game on March 27 (confirmed via NHL schedule)
  • Walk-ins harder than typical Friday due to Auto Show

Transit Reference

  • TransLink trip planner: translink.ca
  • Waterfront = Expo + Millennium + Canada + SeaBus
  • Yaletown-Roundhouse = Canada Line (MeeT, Homer St)
  • Main St-Science World = Expo Line (Torafuku only)
  • SeaBus last Friday ~12:15am
  • SkyTrain last service ~1am most lines

Walk-In Backup Plan

  • Arrive 5:30pm before rush: Guu, La Casita, Belgard
  • Split 2x tables of 5: Guu or Nuba can often accommodate
  • Late option 9pm+: La Casita (1am), Guu or Belgard (midnight)
  • Check Resy + OpenTable morning of March 27 for cancellations
  • Auto Show effect: walk-ins harder than usual in Gastown this Friday
T
Execution Timeline
In strict priority order
  • Today — March 13
    Send dietary survey to guests + contact restaurants now
    Message group asking for dietary restrictions. Email nubagastown@gmail.com and call 604-688-1655 today. Email Homer Street (info@homerstreetcafebar.com) as backup. Call window: 2–5pm. Auto Show adds demand pressure in Gastown — today is the last comfortable day to book.
  • March 14
    Follow up on all contacts + confirm venue based on dietary replies
    If no Nuba reply by noon, call again. Use dietary restriction responses to confirm venue. Halal guest: Nuba is the right call. Vegan-heavy or severe allergies: MeeT Yaletown (dedicated GF fryers, peanut-free facility, full menu dairy/egg free). Mixed group: Nuba handles this cleanly.
  • March 15–16
    Confirm reservation and secure with credit card hold if required
    Get a booking reference number. Disclose all dietary restrictions. Book for the maximum headcount estimate. Ask about group deposit policy.
  • March 18–20
    Send guest confirmation with personalized transit instructions
    Include restaurant address, Google Maps link, arrival time (6:55pm for 7pm reservation), and region-specific transit route. Mention the Auto Show is on at the Convention Centre — Waterfront area may be busier than usual and transit from Waterfront may be slightly more crowded between 5–9pm.
  • March 25
    Call restaurant with final confirmed headcount
    48-hour minimum notice for headcount changes. Confirm exact number, re-confirm dietary restrictions list in writing, ask if any pre-ordering or pre-payment is required for your party.
  • March 27 Morning
    Send reminder with address, maps link, and event heads-up
    "Tonight at 7pm. Address: [X]. Google Maps: [Y]. Arrive by 6:55. FYI — the Auto Show is at the Convention Centre this week so Waterfront area will be a bit busier. Give yourself a few extra minutes from the station."
  • March 27 — 6:50pm
    Arrive early, mention dietary restrictions at the table
    Check in at host stand. Tell your server the dietary restrictions directly — do not assume the kitchen was briefed at booking. Check bill before adding tip — auto-gratuity of 18–20% is common for groups of 6+.
Vancouver Dinner Action Plan — Cycle 3 — March 13, 2026 — Corrections: MeeT Yaletown closes midnight (not 11pm) / Torafuku dinner ends 9:30pm (not 10:30pm) / Auto Show at Convention Centre March 25–29 adds crowd pressure to Waterfront-Gastown area / Monster Jam at PNE March 27 7pm affects eastbound SkyTrain post-dinner — MeeT Gastown closed Nov 2025 — Brix & Mortar March 27 special event unverified (treat as standard venue)
